approach: Revalidate the hash-bound allowlist inversion, three focused branch regressions, pristine inverse control, separate AST truth-table checker, clean pinned-index application, and live prior-art status.

strategy.mechanism: Execute the exact train_best_detector method body with downstream training mocked, compare against pristine addb4a1, and independently parse and evaluate the initial guard.

hypothesis: Changing the guard to `torch_device.type not in ("cuda", "tpu")` makes CPU raise before downstream work while CUDA and TPU reach mocked training.
discriminating_test: Run cpu, cuda, and tpu through the exact method body, replay the opposite outcomes on pristine addb4a1, and separately evaluate the parsed initial guard.

search_efficiency.naive_space: All possible device objects plus the full ML training pipeline; the initial guard itself depends only on membership of torch_device.type in a two-token allowlist.
search_efficiency.chosen_mechanism: Represent the unsupported complement by cpu, retain cuda and tpu as singleton supported cases, compile the exact source method, and mock the two downstream class methods.
search_efficiency.estimated_or_measured_savings: Zero training calls and zero accelerator allocations; three cases ran in 0.003 seconds with 28,436 KiB peak child memory.
search_efficiency.soundness_guard: The guard is the first executable statement and observes only torch_device.type membership; pristine mutation control and a separately written AST/evaluation checker cover the same truth table by different mechanisms.

space_reduction.represented_space_before: The declared supported values {cuda,tpu} and every value outside that set
space_reduction.eliminated_or_quotiented: Every unsupported string is equivalent under the sole membership predicate and is represented by cpu; cuda and tpu remain separate exact cases.
space_reduction.represented_space_after: Three guard-equivalence profiles: cpu, cuda, tpu
space_reduction.reduction_factor: The unbounded unsupported-token complement is quotiented to one class; two supported singleton classes are retained.
space_reduction.coverage_scope: The initial device guard in BayesianDetector.train_best_detector only
space_reduction.soundness_basis: AST inspection proves the first branch contains only `torch_device.type not in ("cuda", "tpu")`; dynamic exact-method execution and independent evaluation agree.
space_reduction.remaining_unknown: Full-package imports and pytest, actual accelerator training, training numerics, upstream CI, and maintainer acceptance are unchecked.
space_reduction.next_bulk_elimination: not applicable; the stated initial-guard branch contract is exhausted

summary: Against live upstream main addb4a1, patch df1d9a58...c1caefd changes `in` to `not in` and adds CPU rejection plus CUDA/TPU acceptance regressions. Run 3 confirmed the pristine inverse bug, passed all three patched cases, passed a separate AST checker, and verified clean index application. No full-package, hardware, training-numerics, CI, or maintainer claim is made.
rationale: The error message explicitly declares CPU unsupported, the guard is the first executable statement, pristine behavior is exactly inverse, and independent dynamic and structural checks agree on all guard-equivalence classes.

The patch applies cleanly to the pristine addb4a1 index and its focused exact-method suite passes 3/3 without accelerator hardware.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol acted as principal investigator. A prior GPT-5.6 Terra source-discriminator memo was advisory and every relied-on claim was independently rechecked. Deterministic work used Python 3.12.3, stdlib ast/unittest, absl-py 2.3.1, mock 5.2.0, git, GitHub REST API, and the Proof Factory experiment runner. No CAS, solver, proof assistant, accelerator, or full ML training stack was used.
